ui.paused_status: Simulation is paused while Settings or Help is open
ui.footer_settings: Settings pauses the simulation. Closing restores the previous running/paused state. Settings are saved to settings/config.json.
ui.footer_help: Up/Down scrolls Help | Enter/Esc returns to Settings | F10/M closes
ui.help_scroll: Help scroll {current}/{max} - use Up/Down or W/S

help.label: Help / key reference
help.desc: Opens the built-in project explanation, option guide and key reference. Settings stays paused while Help is visible.

language.label: Language
language.desc: Selects the UI language. Language files are stored as editable JSON files under language/. English is the default fallback.

resolution.label: Resolution
resolution.desc: Choose a display preset. Press Enter to apply the selected size. 1920 x 1080 Full HD is the default for new installations.

fullscreen.label: Fullscreen / windowed
fullscreen.desc: Switches between fullscreen mode and a normal window. Use windowed mode for diagnostics if the graphics driver has trouble with fullscreen.

view.label: View preset
view.desc: Changes how organisms are colored: classic identity, age glow, energy, family, traits, thermal, ghost and critic heat.

render.label: Render mode
render.desc: Cycles between 2D texture, 3D terrain and true 3D organism-object view. Object mode uses mouse orbit around the organism world.

object_orgs.label: 3D object organism cap
object_orgs.desc: Limits how many primary organisms are generated as true 3D object meshes. Lower values improve performance in object mode.

obj_export_cap.label: OBJ export cap
obj_export_cap.desc: Maximum number of primary organisms written when exporting current lifeforms as OBJ files.

blend.label: Anti-flicker blend
blend.desc: Interpolates between generations to reduce visual flicker. This makes rapid evolution easier to watch.

sim_mode.label: Simulation mode
sim_mode.desc: Organism Mode adds genomes, traits, local bias and evolution. Pure Conway uses only the classic deterministic cellular rules.

speed.label: Simulation speed
speed.desc: Target simulation steps per second. Lower values can help older hardware and make behavior easier to observe.

history_limit.label: Evolution memory depth
history_limit.desc: Controls how many observations each organism keeps as evolutionary memory. Larger values preserve more history, smaller values protect FPS and memory.

dead_archive.label: Dead organism archive cap
dead_archive.desc: Limits how many extinct organisms remain as ancestry/evolution memory. Keeping some history avoids learning only from the current moment.

scan_interval.label: Tracker scan interval
scan_interval.desc: Controls how often the grid is scanned into tracked lifeforms. Higher values reduce CPU cost; lower values track identities more precisely.

memory_status.label: Memory status
memory_status.desc: Shows bounded evolution-memory counters in the detail panel and writes them to the runtime log.

prune_memory.label: Prune memory now
prune_memory.desc: Immediately trims organism histories and the dead archive to the configured caps.

sound.label: Organism soundscape
sound.desc: Optional harmonic sound layer. Important organisms get louder; quieter organisms fade back or drop out when the voice cap is reached.

sound_volume.label: Sound volume
sound_volume.desc: Master volume for generated organism melodies.

sound_voices.label: Sound organism voices
sound_voices.desc: Maximum number of organisms allowed to sing at the same time.

resume_after_menu.label: Resume after closing menu
resume_after_menu.desc: When enabled, a simulation that was running before Settings opened resumes when Settings closes. Settings itself always pauses the world.

critic.label: Critic overlay
critic.desc: Shows or hides the critic heat overlay in render modes that support it.

hud.label: Compact HUD
hud.desc: Shows a compact non-intrusive status line in the main view.

help_overlay.label: F10 hint
help_overlay.desc: Shows only the small F10 Settings hint in the main view.

clean.label: Clean view / hide normal overlays
clean.desc: Hides normal main-view overlays for screenshots or uninterrupted observation.

inject.label: Inject new organism
inject.desc: Adds one generated organism into the world. Left click in the main view injects at the cursor.

export_obj.label: Export OBJ lifeforms
export_obj.desc: Saves the current primary lifeforms as individual OBJ files plus a combined OBJ scene.

reset.label: Reset world
reset.desc: Restarts the world with a fresh generated population.

close.label: Close menu
close.desc: Closes Settings and restores the previous pause state unless resume-after-menu was disabled.

help_lines: What is PandaLife GAN Organism Arena?

This program is an artificial-life sandbox and visualizer. It is inspired by Conway's Game of Life, but it adds organism tracking, genome-like traits, a generator/critic design, evolution memory, 3D views, OBJ export and optional harmonic sound.
What is Game of Life?

Conway's Game of Life is a cellular automaton. A grid of cells is updated step by step. In the classic version, living cells survive with two or three living neighbors; dead cells are born with exactly three living neighbors; otherwise cells die or stay empty.
Why this app exists

Pure Game of Life is deterministic and beautiful, but it has no individual agents. PandaLife keeps a Life-like world and then interprets connected cell clusters as digital organisms. In Organism Mode those organisms receive genomes, traits, local influence and evolutionary scoring so the simulation can feel more like a small ecosystem than random noise.
Main modes
Pure Conway Mode: classic deterministic cellular rules, no organism intervention.
Organism Mode: Life-like rules plus genomes, traits, small local bias, ancestry and scoring.
2D texture: fastest overview of the whole cellular field.
3D terrain: the grid is shown as a height/depth surface.
3D objects: primary organisms are converted into actual x/y/z meshes that can be exported as OBJ snapshots.
Settings menu controls
F10 or M: open/close Settings. The simulation freezes while Settings or Help is open.
F1: open this Help page directly.
Esc: close Help/Settings first; quit only from the main view.
Enter: apply or toggle the selected option / return from Help.
Up/Down or W/S: select Settings items. In Help, scroll this text.
Left/Right or A/D: change the selected Settings value.
Language option

Language switches the menu/help language. Translation files are stored in language/en.json, language/de.json and language/fr.json. You can add or edit JSON files manually; missing keys fall back to English.
Display options
Resolution chooses a display preset. The default is 1920 x 1080 Full HD.
Fullscreen/windowed switches the window mode. Windowed mode is useful for diagnostics.
View preset changes the color interpretation: Classic, Age Glow, Energy, Family, Traits, Thermal, Ghost and Critic Heat.
Render mode cycles 2D texture, 3D terrain and true 3D objects.
3D object organism cap limits how many primary organisms are rendered as full object meshes.
OBJ export cap limits how many primary organisms are exported per OBJ action.
Anti-flicker blend smooths the transition between evolution steps.
Simulation options
Simulation mode switches between Pure Conway and Organism Mode.
Simulation speed sets the target steps per second.
Evolution memory depth controls how many observations each organism remembers.
Dead organism archive cap controls how many extinct organisms remain as ancestry/memory records.
Tracker scan interval controls how often the grid is scanned into tracked lifeforms. Higher is faster; lower is more precise.
Memory status shows current organism/history/archive counters.
Prune memory now immediately trims histories and archive to the configured limits.
Resume after closing menu controls whether a previously running simulation resumes when Settings closes.
Sound options
Organism soundscape enables generated harmonic melodies for important organisms.
Sound volume controls the master volume.
Sound organism voices limits how many organisms can be audible at once. Importance controls loudness.
Overlay options
Critic overlay shows interest/critic scoring when supported.
Compact HUD shows the main status line.
F10 hint shows the small F10 Settings text in the main view.
FPS meter shows Panda3D's frame-rate meter.
Clean view hides normal overlays for screenshots or observation.
Actions
Inject new organism adds one generated organism. In the main view, left-click injects at the mouse cursor.
Export OBJ lifeforms writes primary organisms to exports/obj/ as individual and combined OBJ files.
Reset world restarts with a fresh generated population.
Main-view hotkeys
Space: pause/resume.
R: reset world.
G: inject random organism.
P: toggle Pure Conway / Organism Mode.
+ or =: faster simulation.  -: slower simulation.
V or 1-8: cycle/select view presets.
B: toggle anti-flicker blend.
T: cycle 2D / 3D terrain / 3D object view.
C: toggle critic overlay.
H: toggle compact HUD.
O: toggle clean view.
F: toggle fullscreen/windowed.
E: export primary lifeforms as OBJ files.
F12: save screenshot.
Mouse and camera
Left click: inject organism at cursor if you are not dragging.
Left drag: rotate 3D terrain/object perspective around the lifeform world.
Mouse wheel: zoom in 2D or change orbit distance in 3D/object mode.
WASD/arrows: pan the 2D view or move the 3D orbit target when Settings is closed.
Files and output
settings/config.json stores persistent settings.
logs/ contains runtime logs. logs/install/ contains installer logs.
exports/obj/ receives exported lifeform meshes.
screenshots/ receives F12 screenshots.
language/ contains editable translation JSON files.
